title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 November 2025.

items.title: Valve accidentally ruins a game's (**Planet Centauri**) sales again after apologizing for ruining its sales the first time
bullets: Originally, Valve screwed up sending out notifications to anyone who had wishlisted Planet Centauri when it came out of early access. As a consolation prize Valve offered the devs the opportunity to put their game on the front page in the “daily deals” section and gave them the option to select from some dates. The devs selected November 12th. This also happened to be the announcement day of the new Steam Machine, Steam Frame, and Steam Controller. The entire front page of the store got squished down to make way for the big announcement, essentially knocking Planet Centauri accidentally out of the way.

items.title: ‘I don’t think we qualify’: **Megabonk** dev withdraws nomination from The Game Awards. Developer claims Megabonk doesn't count as a "debut" indie game as “I’ve made games in the past under different studio names, so Megabonk is not my debut game”
Nier Director **Yoko Taro** assures players he’s been working on projects, but they keep getting cancelled

items.title: Bungie settles **Destiny 2** copyright lawsuit with writer in undisclosed settlement. If no further objections are made within 60 days, the court will presume "all parties have firmly agreed upon a compromise"
**Farming Simulator 25** surpasses four million copies sold
Riot sells **Hytale** IP and Hypixel brand back to original co-founder
